Package com.company

Class inputRow

java.lang.Object
java.awt.Component
java.awt.Container
javax.swing.JComponent
javax.swing.JPanel
com.company.inputRow
All Implemented Interfaces:
java.awt.image.ImageObserver, java.awt.MenuContainer, java.io.Serializable, javax.accessibility.Accessible
Direct Known Subclasses:
inputRowBook, inputRowConference, inputRowFilm, inputRowJournal, inputRowPeriodical, inputRowWebsite
public abstract class inputRow
extends javax.swing.JPanel
inputRow is an abstract class which means it's never used directly. It is template used by child classes. It creates an empty input row ready for unique fields to be added.
Version:
1.0.0
Author:
Peter Bedford
See Also:
Serialized Form

  • Method Details

    • closeRow

      private void closeRow()
      Closes the input row, deletes it from rowArray and updates the GUI. Called by the close button.

    • buildInputs

      public void buildInputs​(java.lang.String[] attributeInput)
      Itterates over the strings in attributeInput adding input JLabels and JTextFields to inputRowBodycol1
      Parameters:
      attributeInput - is an array of input field names.

    • updatePreview

      public void updatePreview()
      If the data is valid a preview of the referene is added to referencePreviewLabel.

    • getRowID

      public int getRowID()
      Used to get the row ID.
      Returns:
      row ID set when creating the row.

    • setAttributeData

      public void setAttributeData​(int attributeID, java.lang.String attributeContent)
      Stores attribute string data in the attributeData array at an index of attributeID.
      Parameters:
      attributeContent - Attribute text data.
      attributeID - Attribute ID used for identification.

    • getAttributeData

      public java.lang.String getAttributeData​(int attributeID)
      Retrieves attribute string data from the attributeData array at an index of attributeID.
      Parameters:
      attributeID - Attribute ID used for identification.
      Returns:
      Attribute text data.

    • validationSuccess

      public void validationSuccess​(int i)
      Resets label text from red to black after validation has succeded and stores the data.
      Parameters:
      i - the ID of the input label.

    • validationFailure

      public boolean validationFailure​(int i)
      Sets label text to red after validation has failed.
      Parameters:
      i - the ID of the input label.
      Returns:
      always returns true.

    • validationGetErrorString

      public java.lang.String validationGetErrorString​(java.lang.String attributeTitle)
      If a field fails validation this returns the relevent error message.
      Parameters:
      attributeTitle - Used to look up which error message is needed.
      Returns:
      The error string.

    • formatName

      public java.lang.String formatName​(java.lang.String nameIn)
      Formats first and last names if both names are supplied, otherwise uses single name.
      Parameters:
      nameIn - Name data from the input field.
      Returns:
      Formatted name string.

    • validateData

      public abstract boolean validateData()
      Checks if all data in the row is valid.
      Returns:
      returns boolean for validation success or failure.

    • generateReferences

      public abstract java.lang.String generateReferences()
      Extracts attribute data and formats it into a reference.
      Returns:
      The final formatted reference.
